# \# XAI Counterfactual Explanations Dashboard

# 

# An interactive Streamlit dashboard for exploring and evaluating image-based counterfactual (CF) explanations. Built for course JM0110 — Interactive \& Explainable AI Design at JADS.

# 

# The dashboard lets you browse CF explanations generated by 5 different methods on MNIST and CIFAR-10 datasets, and run a user study to test whether human intuition aligns with objective evaluation metrics.

# 

# \---

# 

# \## Research Question

# 

# > To what extent do objective evaluation metrics (validity, plausibility) align with users' intuitive judgements of counterfactual explanation quality?

# 

# \---

# 

# \## Files

# 

# | File | Description |

# |---|---|

# | `app.py` | Main Streamlit dashboard application |

# | `data\_utils.py` | Data loading utilities, image loaders, tension case finder |

# | `guess\_log.json` | Auto-generated when the first Guess Mode response is recorded — stores all user study responses |

# 

# \---

# 

# \## Data Setup

# 

# The data is \*\*not included\*\* in this repository (confidential, paper under review). You need to set it up yourself.

# 

# Create a `Data/` folder in the project root with the following structure:

# 

# ```

# Data/

# ├── evaluation\_results.csv

# ├── mnist\_output/

# │   ├── original/

# │   │   └── instance\_{id}.pt

# │   ├── PIECE/

# │   ├── Min-Edit/

# │   ├── C-Min-Edit/

# │   ├── alibi-Proto-CF/

# │   └── alibi-CF/

# └── cifar\_resnet8\_output/

# &#x20;   └── cifar\_resnet8\_output/        ← note: nested folder

# &#x20;       ├── original/

# &#x20;       ├── PIECE/

# &#x20;       ├── Min-Edit/

# &#x20;       ├── C-Min-Edit/

# &#x20;       ├── alibi-Proto-CF/

# &#x20;       └── alibi-CF/

# ```

# 

# > \*\*Important:\*\* The CIFAR folder has an extra nested level (`cifar\_resnet8\_output/cifar\_resnet8\_output/`). Make sure to replicate this exactly.

# 

# \---

# 

# \## Installation

# 

# ```bash

# pip install streamlit torch numpy pandas matplotlib

# ```

# 

# \---

# 

# \## Running the Dashboard

# 

# ```bash

# streamlit run app.py

# ```

# 

# \---

# 

# \## Dashboard Tabs

# 

# \- \*\*Overview\*\* — Validity rates, plausibility distributions and timeout rates across all methods

# \- \*\*Image Browser\*\* — Browse original images and their 5 CF explanations side by side with metric scores

# \- \*\*Tension Cases\*\* — Automatically surfaces cases where validity and plausibility disagree most strongly

# \- \*\*Guess Mode\*\* — User study feature: guess whether the model considers a CF valid, then see the truth. Responses are logged to `guess\_log.json`

# \- \*\*Study Results\*\* — Live analysis of collected Guess Mode responses, with accuracy by method and download option

# 

# \---

# 

# \## CF Methods

# 

# | Method | Notes |

# |---|---|

# | PIECE | Makes small pixel changes, often visually natural but may not fool the model |

# | Min-Edit | Minimises pixel changes, can look unnatural |

# | C-Min-Edit | Constrained version of Min-Edit |

# | alibi-Proto-CF | Prototype-based, tends to produce noisy results |

# | alibi-CF | Can time out and produce no result |

# 

# \---

# 

# \## CIFAR-10 Class Labels

# 

# `0=airplane, 1=automobile, 2=bird, 3=cat, 4=deer, 5=dog, 6=frog, 7=horse, 8=ship, 9=truck`
